references.reason: An airwell lets the library's elemental logistics become a visible, ventilated problem.
A dust ward makes careful library maintenance actionable without turning Patchouli's limits into a gag.
An elemental shelf supports precise questions and safe placement.
The first route observation records effort budgeting and a respected pause.
An annotation table makes the quality and scope of a question visible.
A margin records an exact question without demanding an immediate answer.
A closed catalog makes trivia and access boundaries explicit.
The journal records curiosity that respects a question’s scope and the right not to answer.
A two-seat alcove permits shared reading without requiring shared conversation.
Separate bookmarks show that the readers may keep different places and different books.
A visible switch makes disabled companion comments an authored boundary rather than missing feedback.
The journal records that quiet attention can be shared without becoming a performance.
A return desk gives Patchouli and Marisa a concrete place to negotiate access and responsibility.
The ledger records due care without treating a borrower as a caricature.
Annotations show Marisa’s research work and the risks of untracked access.
The journal records a library rule that protects both research and responsibility.
